message:
fix(core,server): session snapshot isolation, duplicate request rejection, HTTP cancellation responsiveness, and pre-failed task short-circuit

Session State Isolation:
- New SessionState::snapshot() creates a deep-copied clone (unlike Clone
  which shares Arc<Mutex>), preventing write bleed across concurrent requests
- SessionSnapshot::from_session now uses snapshot() instead of clone()
- Test confirms write isolation between original and snapshot

Duplicate Request ID Rejection:
- ActiveRequestGuard::new renamed to try_new, returns Result<Self, RequestId>
- Returns Err if request ID is already active instead of silently replacing
- Both handle_request paths return InvalidRequest JSON-RPC error for duplicates
- Tests verify rejection behavior

HTTP Server Cancellation Responsiveness:
- HTTP listener switched from blocking to nonblocking mode so cancellation
  and shutdown are observed promptly without needing a dummy connection to
  wake accept()
- Accepted sockets explicitly set back to blocking mode for request/response
- Test validates prompt shutdown without accept wakeup

Pre-Failed Task Short-Circuit:
- spawn_task returns early (skipping handler) when running snapshot is None,
  which happens when a task has already transitioned to a terminal state
  before execution begins
- Test: spawn_task_skips_handler_for_pre_failed_pending_task

HTTP Auth Isolation:
- End-to-end test overlaps two concurrent HTTP requests with different auth
  tokens via barrier and asserts each sees only its own token

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
